steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes the word "suggesting that ______" — the conclusion must follow logically from the evidence. The passage says most studies outside North America found the same positive association between dissolved organic carbon and mercury, while few matched Porvari and Verta's no-correlation result. The correct answer reflects what the majority of evidence actually shows.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — the hypothesis that the positive association is particular to North America is correct.: contradicted directly — multiple non-North-American studies showed the same positive association, so it is not particular to North America. ✗
  • Choice B — levels of dissolved organic carbon and mercury in bodies of fresh water are both much higher in Finland than elsewhere.: the passage says nothing about absolute levels in Finland; it only describes whether the two variables correlate. ✗
  • Choice C — there were circumstances unique to Ekstrom and colleagues' study that impeded accurate measurements of mercury levels.: the passage presents Ekstrom's study as supporting the majority view, not as flawed. ✗
  • Choice D — dissolved organic carbon and mercury levels do typically rise and fall together in fresh water.: most studies globally found the positive association; that is exactly what "typically" captures. ✓
That matches Choice D — dissolved organic carbon and mercury levels do typically rise and fall together in fresh water..
